title: Dungeon Degenerates - Let's Play - Establishing a Base - Part 3
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lZm2ZwjEy38

Daniel (@dungeondive) corrects his skill selections—the Looter card doesn't work for Fighters, so he pivots to Ferocious Charge, which grants extra dice on strength attacks in assault stance. The Bloodsport Brawler's first few turns are plagued by brutal dice rolls. Strength checks that should hit come up snake eyes, and she burns through luck rerolls just to keep the party alive.

The encounter ramps up when other monsters are summoned mid-fight. Now facing a swamp-dwelling horde of fish-themed freaks—lesser scrogs, fish-eyed warlocks with curse abilities—Daniel's heroes get genuinely tested. The Brawler tanks damage while demoralized, hovering near death, and the Vermin Hunter becomes the party's true MVP, her consistent rolls keeping both of them alive through some absolutely devastating combat rounds.

Eventually, they survive the encounter and lower the Sunken Village's danger level. This triggers the Stone Circle's town level increase—one step closer to establishing their base. Daniel invests 20 gold pieces directly into the settlement, raising it toward level four. The grind-style gameplay proves surprisingly engaging, with each victory inching them closer to safety.

----

**What's your tolerance for brutal luck swings in solo campaigns?**
